confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_b9445f9a-7763-46e2-8a53-33bdf58154c6 read back as promoted

payload.body:
# File-Drop Request Kind Is Admitted As Observation

## Summary

While executing the thoughts Project Site daemon-source tasks, a canonical file-drop item with front matter `kind: request` was dry-run inspected and admitted through `narada inbox ingest-files`.

The resulting envelope was admitted as `kind: observation`. The original `kind: request` survived only inside `payload.front_matter.kind`.

## Evidence

- Source item: `/home/andrey/src/thoughts/.narada/.ai/inbox-drop/20260428-001-request-return-message.md`
- Front matter included `kind: request`.
- Dry-run output reported `kind: observation`.
- Admit output created `env_303f3f70-77a3-4a99-9ed4-9cedff8e5d5f` with `kind: observation`.
- `inbox show` confirmed payload front matter preserved `kind: request`.

## Proposal

Decide whether human file-drop front matter should be able to set canonical envelope kinds beyond `observation`.

If yes, update `inbox ingest-files` so supported front-matter kinds such as `request` are admitted as that canonical kind, with validation and rejection for unsupported kinds.

If no, document explicitly that file-drop intake always admits observation envelopes and that request/proposal semantics live inside payload/front matter until triage.

## Recommendation

Treat this as a small canonical-inbox adapter dearbitrization task. The current behavior is workable but surprising during inhabited use.

front_matter.title: File-drop request kind is admitted as observation
